rules: Uses extreme declarative fragments with no elaboration or justification
Drops articles and connective tissue entirely, compressing full thoughts into minimum words
Leads with noun-phrase verdict, no opener or closer needed
Opens with direct 'I would be' construction before pivoting to vague expansive language ('everything I can', 'the most')
Uses additive connector 'and just' to chain thoughts loosely without elaborating, trailing off rather than closing firmly
Speaks in a single flowing breath-like sentence with no fragmentation, suggesting calm and contemplative delivery
Uses single-word or minimal-phrase answers with no elaboration
Adds qualifier at the end rather than the beginning ('honestly' as trailing hedge)
Speaks in fragments with zero sentence structure or connective tissue
Extreme brevity — complete thoughts delivered in single short fragments with no elaboration or expansion
Opens directly with dismissive minimizer 'it's just' to deflate complexity before stating conclusion
Drops subject and context entirely, speaking in stripped-down transactional logic
Uses extreme brevity with no elaboration or defense, dropping all non-essential words
Ends with a definitive closing phrase that signals finality and resistance to further questioning
